items.description: No card numbers, CVV, or PAN in any log, database, cache, or error message
Payment processor webhook signatures verified with HMAC-SHA256 and replay protection
PCI scope clearly defined and documented — CDE boundaries explicit
Payment-adjacent systems network-segmented from non-payment systems
Complete audit trail maintained for all payment operations — tamper-evident logs
Raw card data never stored — tokenization used throughout
TLS 1.2+ required on all payment data flows — no fallback to older protocols
Strong cryptography used — no weak ciphers, MD5, SHA1 for security purposes
Access to payment data restricted to minimum necessary roles — least privilege
Payment system dependencies scanned — no CRITICAL vulnerabilities unresolved
WAF in place and tuned for payment endpoints — OWASP rule sets active
IDS/IPS monitoring payment data flows with alerting configured
File integrity monitoring on payment system files — alerts on unauthorized change
Payment processing handled by PCI-compliant vendor (Stripe/Braintree/Adyen) — not custom
Penetration test conducted within the last 12 months for payment scope
Anti-fraud controls in place — velocity checks, geographic anomaly detection
Chargeback monitoring and alerting configured with defined response process
Payment data retention policy enforced — data purged per PCI DSS schedule
Payment fraud and PCI breach IR playbooks exist and are current
Threat model completed and reviewed for this payment surface change
Magecart/digital-skimming prevention: SRI hashes on all checkout page scripts; CSP blocks unauthorized exfiltration destinations; DOM mutation monitoring detects injected form skimmers
EMV 3D Secure 2.2+ enforced for card-not-present transactions above risk threshold; step-up authentication triggered by anomaly score; 3DS bypass attempts logged and alerted
